_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: An entire nation had to be tricked into believing in her before she could become real.

museum_objects.title: Peça da Coroação (Coronation Coin of Pedro I)
museum_objects.description: An extremely rare commemorative gold coin from Pedro I's coronation, with only 64 ever minted.
museum_objects.significance: Nearly two centuries before Fernanda existed, Brazil's very first independent ruler was already trying to prove his new nation was worth something in gold.
museum_objects.museum: Museu de Valores, Banco Central do Brasil
museum_objects.plain_language: An extremely rare gold coin made for the coronation of Brazil's first emperor in 1822 — only 64 were ever made.
museum_objects.institution: Museu de Valores, Banco Central do Brasil

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

display_alternatives.title: Moeda a coroação de Pedro I 1822.png
Stamp of Brazil - 1982 - Colnect 261428 - Pedro I Coronation piece.jpeg

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so.

museum_objects.title: Unidade Real de Valor (URV) Documentation
museum_objects.description: Records of the URV, a purely virtual accounting unit used to psychologically recalibrate prices before the real's physical launch.
museum_objects.significance: Fernanda existed as an idea before she existed as money — an entire country had to practice believing in her before she was allowed to be real.
museum_objects.plain_language: Records of a strange virtual currency Brazil used for a few months in 1994 to trick people's minds into trusting stable prices, before the real physically existed.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Collor Plan Confiscation Photograph
museum_objects.description: A photograph of Economy Minister Zélia Cardoso de Mello announcing the 1990 freeze on Brazilian savings accounts.
museum_objects.significance: Before Fernanda could be trusted, Brazil had to go through this — an entire generation watching their savings get frozen overnight by the government.
museum_objects.plain_language: A photo from 1990 showing the government official who announced Brazil would freeze everyone's savings accounts overnight.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Museu de Valores Numismatic Collection
museum_objects.description: Brazil's central bank museum, home to over 125,000 numismatic items spanning the country's entire monetary history.
museum_objects.significance: Every failed currency that came before Fernanda — cruzeiros, cruzados, and more — sits in this collection, a graveyard of broken promises she had to overcome.
museum_objects.museum: Museu de Valores, Banco Central do Brasil
museum_objects.accession_number: Museu de Valores general collection (125,000+ items)
museum_objects.plain_language: Brazil's central bank museum, holding over 125,000 historical money items, including all the failed currencies that came before the real.
museum_objects.accession: Museu de Valores general collection (125,000+ items)
museum_objects.institution: Museu de Valores, Banco Central do Brasil

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Plano Real Official Launch Documentation
museum_objects.description: Official documentation of the Plano Real, the economic stabilization plan that introduced the Brazilian real.
museum_objects.significance: This is the day Fernanda finally became real — after years of hyperinflation and five failed currencies, Brazil finally got one that stuck.
museum_objects.plain_language: The official launch documents for Brazil's current currency, the real, introduced in 1994 after decades of economic chaos.

world_map.map_description: Fernanda was carefully engineered into existence through the Plano Real after decades of hyperinflation, and has since anchored Latin America's largest economy.

nodes.label: Brasília (BCB Museum)
nodes.description: Brazil's capital, home to the central bank and its Museu de Valores documenting the real's creation.

nodes.label: Rio de Janeiro
nodes.description: Brazil's cultural capital, symbol of both the country's wealth and deep inequality that shaped economic reform debates.

nodes.label: São Paulo
nodes.description: Brazil's financial capital and largest city, central to the real's role in the modern economy.

nodes.label: Amazon Rainforest
nodes.description: A defining natural resource shaping Brazil's economic identity and global trade relationships.

nodes.label: Pantanal
nodes.description: Brazil's vast wetland region, part of the agricultural economy that underpins the real's stability.

historical_map_source.title: Banco Central do Brasil — Real Plan
